message:
fix(scheduler): harden local task safety, deadline dispatch, panic recovery, and counter underflow protection

A collection of correctness and robustness improvements across the
scheduler subsystem, addressing several interrelated issues:

Local task safety (steal ordering + rejection):
- IntrusiveStack: add push_bottom() to reinsert tasks at the bottom of
  the stack, preserving owner-visible LIFO order when steal probes
  temporarily remove local (!Send) tasks during scanning
- Stealer::steal/steal_batch: collect skipped local tasks in a Vec and
  restore them via push_bottom in reverse order after the scan completes,
  instead of pushing them back to the top (which inverted ordering after
  repeated failed steal probes)
- ThreeLaneWorker::schedule_local/schedule_local_timed: replace debug_assert
  with runtime error!() + return false for local tasks, preventing local
  tasks from silently entering stealable PriorityScheduler or timed lanes
  even when debug assertions are disabled

Deadline-aware dispatch:
- PriorityScheduler: add pop_with_lane_if_due(rng, now) and
  pop_non_cancel_with_rng_if_due(rng, now) that check timed lane peek
  deadline before popping, falling through to the ready lane when timed
  tasks are not yet due (prevents premature dispatch of future timers)
- Add doc comments on existing deadline-agnostic methods pointing to the
  new if_due variants

Decision contract fix:
- Clamp deadline_pressure to [0.0, 1.0] once (as deadline_signal) instead
  of calling .min(1.0) repeatedly
- Fix partitioned_score formula: was incorrectly diluted by its own
  denominator, now uses deadline_signal * (0.5 + cancel_drain_ratio)
  which correctly makes partitioned dominate when both deadline pressure
  and cancel/drain activity are elevated
- Add tests for high deadline pressure and combined deadline+cancel pressure

Panic unwind safety (worker.rs):
- Add TaskExecutionGuard RAII struct that catches unwind in Drop:
  transitions the panicking task to Panicked terminal state, runs
  task_completed to collect waiters, wakes local and global waiters,
  and drains ready async finalizers, preventing obligation leaks and
  stuck dependents when a task panics during poll

Counter underflow protection (global_injector.rs):
- Replace fetch_sub(1) with fetch_update + checked_sub for pending_count
  and ready_count decrements, preventing underflow to usize::MAX when
  a pop races with a lagging inject counter update
- Reorder inject methods to increment counter before pushing to the
  concurrent queue, ensuring the counter is never behind the queue length
  (visible-before-counted invariant)
- Add test verifying pop does not underflow when counter lags

Zero-worker forward progress (mod.rs):
- Clamp worker_count to max(1) in WorkStealingScheduler::new to prevent
  a zero-worker scheduler that can never make progress

guard.completed placement (three_lane.rs):
- Move guard.completed = true to after state transitions and waiter waking
  in both Poll::Ready and Poll::Pending branches, ensuring the completion
  guard is only disarmed after all side effects have been applied

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
